### Setup Script for bun with Convex Dev

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/overview

This script uses bun to install dependencies and start a Convex development server, suitable for background agents. It requires full internet access to download the Convex binary.

```bash
bun i

bun x convex dev --once
```

--------------------------------

### Run Example App with Component Type Checking

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/components/authoring

Starts the development server for the example app, optionally performing type checking on integrated components. This command should be run after component codegen and package build.

```bash
npx convex dev --typecheck-components # optionally type-check the components
```

--------------------------------

### Clone and run RAG example project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/rag

Setup and execute the RAG example to demonstrate adding content, searching, generating text, and introspecting context with various filter options.

```Bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/rag.git

cd rag

npm run setup

npm run example
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start app with Convex template

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Quick setup command to scaffold a new TanStack Start project with Convex pre-configured.

```bash
npm create convex@latest -- -t tanstack-start
```

--------------------------------

### Install AuthKit TanStack Start SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

Install the AuthKit SDK for TanStack React Start using npm.

```bash
npm install @workos/authkit-tanstack-react-start
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Convex Tutorial Project and Install Dependencies

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/tutorial/overview

Clone the example project from GitHub and install its dependencies using npm. Ensure Node.js 18+ and Git are installed.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/convex-tutorial.git

cd convex-tutorial

npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Script for npm with Convex Dev

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/overview

Use this script to install dependencies and start a Convex development server in a way that's suitable for background agents. It includes an optional step to set environment variables from a file.

```bash
npm i

# npx convex init # allows setting environment variables before pushing

# npx convex env set --from-file ./path/to/.env.agent (optional)

npx convex dev --once
```

--------------------------------

### Get URL and Metadata for Stored File (Initial Example)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageReader

This example demonstrates how to get a URL for a stored file and retrieve its metadata using the system table, which is the preferred method.

```javascript
// Get a URL for a stored file:

const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);

if (url) {

  // Use the URL (e.g., return it to the client)

}



// Get file metadata via the system table (preferred over deprecated getMetadata):

const metadata = await ctx.db.system.get("_storage", storageId);

// metadata: { _id, _creationTime, sha256, size, contentType? }
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Project with Convex Auth

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/convex-auth

Use this command to start a new project from scratch, selecting 'React (Vite)' and 'Convex Auth' during the setup process.

```bash
npm create convex@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Client and Server

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/swift

Install the Convex client and server libraries using npm. This is a prerequisite for starting the Convex dev deployment.

```bash
npm init -y

npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Basic HTTP Router Setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/http-actions

Sets up a basic HTTP router with a single GET route at the root path. This is the entry point for HTTP actions.

```typescript
import {
  httpRouter,
} from "convex/server";

import {
  httpAction,
} from "./_generated/server";



const http = httpRouter();



http.route({
  path: "/",
  method: "GET",
  handler: httpAction(async (ctx, request) => {
    return new Response(`Hello from ${request.url}`);
  }),
});

export default http;
```

--------------------------------

### Basic HTTP Router Setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/http-actions

This snippet shows how to set up a basic HTTP router and define a simple GET route at the root path.

```APIDOC
## GET /

### Description
Handles GET requests to the root path.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/

### Handler
```typescript
import { httpRouter } from "convex/server";
import { httpAction } from "./_generated/server";

const http = httpRouter();

http.route({
  path: "/",
  method: "GET",
  handler: httpAction(async (ctx, request) => {
    return new Response(`Hello from ${request.url}`);
  }),
});

export default http;
```
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Script for Cloud Codex with Convex

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/using-codex

This script is suitable for setting up Codex in cloud environments like ChatGPT. It installs dependencies and starts a local Convex backend for agent mode.

```bash
npm i

npx convex dev --once
```

--------------------------------

### Run Convex Agent Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/files

This snippet provides the commands to clone, set up, and run the Convex agent example project locally.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/agent.git

cd agent

npm run setup

npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/chef/migration-guide

Navigate to your project folder and install all necessary dependencies using npm.

```bash
cd ~/<app folder>

npm i
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Client and Server Libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/svelte

Navigates into the newly created project directory and installs the `convex` and `convex-svelte` packages required for integration.

```bash
cd my-app && npm install convex convex-svelte
```

--------------------------------

### Start MCP Server

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/mcp

Starts the Model Context Protocol server for Convex, enabling AI tool integration.

```bash
npx convex mcp start [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start app

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Initialize a new TanStack Start application using the official create-start-app command.

```bash
npx create-start-app@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Starting a Frontend Dev Server with `npx convex dev`

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/dev

Use the `--start` option to run a long-running command, such as a frontend development server, alongside `npx convex dev`. The command inherits stdin/stdout for interaction.

```bash
npx convex dev --start 'vite --open'
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex and Nuxt module

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nuxt

Installs the Convex client library and the `convex-nuxt` module into your project.

```bash
cd my-nuxt-app && npm install convex && npx nuxi module add convex-nuxt
```

--------------------------------

### Start the React Native App

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/react-native

Run this command to start your React Native application.

```bash
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex and React Query dependencies

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Install the Convex client library along with React Query and TanStack Router SSR integration packages required for TanStack Start.

```bash
npm install convex @convex-dev/react-query @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query @tanstack/react-query
```

### Common Android Imports

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/android

Example import declarations for common Android Jetpack Compose and Activity components used in the quickstart.

```kotlin
import android.os.Bundle

import androidx.activity.ComponentActivity

import androidx.activity.compose.setContent

import androidx.activity.enableEdgeToEdge

import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize

import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding

import androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.LazyColumn

import androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.items
```

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/tanstack/tanstack-query

Install the adapter library using npm. This is the first step to enable Convex integration with TanStack Query.

```bash
npm i @convex-dev/react-query
```

Install Vitest, convex-test, and @edge-runtime/vm for testing Convex functions.

```bash
npm install --save-dev convex-test vitest @edge-runtime/vm
```

Install the Convex package into your Remix application.

```bash
cd my-remix-app && npm install convex
```

### Start Convex Application in Dev Mode

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit

After creating a new project, run this command to start the application in development mode, which initiates the Convex+WorkOS onboarding flow.

```bash
cd my-app

npm run dev
```

### Install Convex client and dotenv

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nodejs

Installs the `convex` package for interacting with Convex and `dotenv` for loading environment variables.

```bash
npm install convex dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Example push_config Audit Log

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/integrations/log-streams/legacy-event-schema

This example shows the structure of an audit log entry for a 'push_config' action, detailing added and removed modules.

```json
{

  "_topic": "_audit_log",

  "_timestamp": 1695066350531,

  "action": "push_config",

  "actionMetadata": {

    "modules": {

      "added": ["ffmpeg.js", "fetch.js", "test.js"],

      "removed": ["removed.js"]

    }

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### Setup ConvexQueryClient and QueryClient

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/tanstack/tanstack-query

Configure Convex and TanStack Query clients in your main application file. This setup ensures live updates by connecting Convex to TanStack Query.

```typescript
import { ConvexQueryClient } from "@convex-dev/react-query";

import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query";

import { ConvexProvider, ConvexReactClient } from "convex/react";

import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";

import App from "./App";

import "./index.css";



const convex = new ConvexReactClient(import.meta.env.VITE_CONVEX_URL);

const convexQueryClient = new ConvexQueryClient(convex);

const queryClient = new QueryClient({

  defaultOptions: {

    queries: {

      queryKeyHashFn: convexQueryClient.hashFn(),

      queryFn: convexQueryClient.queryFn(),

    },

  },

});

convexQueryClient.connect(queryClient);



ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render(

  <ConvexProvider client={convex}>

    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>

      <App />

    </QueryClientProvider>

  </ConvexProvider>,

);
```

--------------------------------

### Start Convex MCP Server

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/convex-mcp-server

Add this command to your MCP server's configuration to start the Convex MCP server.

```bash
npx -y convex@latest mcp start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex client and server libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/rust

Installs the Convex npm package for backend development and the Convex Rust client library, Tokio runtime, and dotenvy for environment variable management.

```bash
npm init -y && npm install convex && cargo add convex tokio dotenvy
```

--------------------------------

### Install AuthKit Next.js SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

This command installs the necessary AuthKit Next.js SDK and the Convex WorkOS integration package. Run this in your project's terminal to add the dependencies.

```bash
npm install @workos-inc/authkit-nextjs @convex-dev/workos
```

--------------------------------

### Install Clerk Next.js SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/clerk

Install the Clerk Next.js SDK in your project by running the following npm command in a new terminal window.

```bash
npm install @clerk/nextjs
```

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/script-tag

Use `mkdir` and `npm init` to set up a new project directory.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && npm init -y
```

### Install AuthKit React SDK and Convex WorkOS Package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

Install the necessary AuthKit React SDK and the Convex WorkOS integration package using npm.

```bash
npm install @workos-inc/authkit-react @convex-dev/workos
```

### Install Convex client and server library

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Add the `convex` package to your Bun project dependencies.

```bash
bun add convex
```

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/python

Installs the Convex npm package for backend development and the Convex Python client library along with python-dotenv for environment variables.

```bash
cd my-app && npm init -y && npm install convex && venv/bin/pip install convex python-dotenv
```

### Set up Convex development deployment

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Run this command to log in, create a Convex project, and sync your backend functions with the dev deployment.

```bash
bunx convex dev
```

### Install Convex Agent Skills

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/agent-skills

Use the `npx skills` CLI to add Convex agent skills to your project. You can choose specific skills or install all of them.

```bash
npx skills add get-convex/agent-skills
```

```bash
npx skills add get-convex/agent-skills --all
```

--------------------------------

### Open Specific Deployment by Name

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/dashboard

Example of opening a dashboard for a deployment identified by its name.

```bash
npx convex dashboard --deployment joyful-capybara-123
```

--------------------------------

### Example Current Storage Usage Event

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/integrations/log-streams

An example of a `current_storage_usage` event, showing the schema for reporting storage statistics. This event is useful for monitoring and calculating billing costs.

```json
{

  "topic": "current_storage_usage",

  "timestamp": 1715973841548,

  "total_document_size_bytes": 104857600,

  "total_index_size_bytes": 10485760,

  "total_vector_storage_bytes": 5242880,

  "total_text_storage_bytes": 1048576,

  "total_file_storage_bytes": 52428800,

  "total_backup_storage_bytes": 209715200,

  "total_system_table_document_size_bytes": {

    "_storage": 1048576,

    "_scheduled_functions": 2097152

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Dev Deployment and Select

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/deployment

Creates a new development deployment and selects it for use. This updates `.env.local`.

```bash
npx convex deployment create dev/my-new-feature --type dev --select
```

--------------------------------

### Query Function to Get Data from Database

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/query-functions

An example of an asynchronous query function that uses the `ctx.db.get()` method to fetch a document from the 'tasks' table by its ID. Requires `async/await`.

```typescript
import { query } from "./_generated/server";

import { v } from "convex/values";



export const getTask = query({

  args: { id: v.id("tasks") },

  handler: async (ctx, args) => {

    return await ctx.db.get("tasks", args.id);

  },

});
```

--------------------------------

### Project Token Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types

Illustrates the format of a project token, which grants full control over a project to a Convex CLI.

```text
project:team-slug:project-slug|eyJ2...0=
```

--------------------------------

### GET /getMessagesByAuthor - Retrieve Messages by Author

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/classes/server.HttpRouter

Example HTTP action that retrieves messages filtered by author. This endpoint demonstrates importing HTTP action handlers from separate files.

```APIDOC
## GET /getMessagesByAuthor

### Description
Retrieves messages filtered by a specific author. This endpoint demonstrates the pattern of importing HTTP action handlers from separate module files.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/getMessagesByAuthor

### Implementation Pattern
```javascript
// In convex/http.js
import { getMessagesByAuthor } from "./getMessagesByAuthor";

http.route({
  path: "/getMessagesByAuthor",
  method: "GET",
  handler: getMessagesByAuthor,
});

// Handler is defined in convex/getMessagesByAuthor.js
```

### Notes
- Handler is imported from a separate file (getMessagesByAuthor.js)
- Demonstrates modular organization of HTTP action handlers
- Actual query parameters and response format depend on the imported handler implementation
```

--------------------------------

### Initial Query Result

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/understanding/overview

This is an example of the initial data returned by the `getAllOpenTasks` query when no tasks have been completed.

```json
[

  { _id: "e4g", title: "Grocery shopping", complete: false },

  { _id: "u9v", title: "Plant new flowers", complete: false },

];
```

--------------------------------

### `useSmoothText` with Conditional Streaming

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/streaming

This example shows how to conditionally start streaming text immediately based on a message's status. It's useful for mixed streaming and non-streaming messages.

```typescript
import { useSmoothText, type UIMessage } from "@convex-dev/agent/react";



function Message({ message }: { message: UIMessage }) {

  const [visibleText] = useSmoothText(message.text, {

    startStreaming: message.status === "streaming",

  });

  return <div>{visibleText}</div>;

}
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Use NPM Packages in Mutations

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/mutation-functions

Mutations can import and utilize packages from node_modules. Ensure the package is supported by the Convex runtime. This example uses `@faker-js/faker` to generate random data.

```bash
npm install @faker-js/faker
```

```typescript
import { faker } from "@faker-js/faker";

import { mutation } from "./_generated/server";



export const randomName = mutation({
  args: {},
  handler: async (ctx) => {
    faker.seed();
    await ctx.db.insert("tasks", { text: "Greet " + faker.person.fullName() });
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a New Convex Project with Codex

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/using-codex

Use this sequence to let Codex create a new Convex project from scratch. It handles package installation, Convex AI file setup, and local backend provisioning.

```bash
npm create convex@latest my-app

cd my-app

npx convex ai-files install

codex
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Usage of `npx convex dev`

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/dev

Run this command in your project directory to start the Convex development server. It configures the project if necessary, pushes code, and watches for changes.

```bash
npx convex dev [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Convex Client and MainActivity

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/android

Initialize the ConvexClient with your Convex deployment URL and set up the MainActivity to display the Tasks Composable.

```kotlin
val client: ConvexClient by lazy { ConvexClient($YOUR_CONVEX_URL) }



class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {

  override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {

      super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)

      enableEdgeToEdge()

      setContent {

          ConvexQuickstartTheme {

              Scaffold(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) { innerPadding ->

                  Tasks(

                      modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding)

                  )

              }

          }

      }

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### GET ctx.storage.getUrl

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageActionWriter

Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

```APIDOC
## GET ctx.storage.getUrl

### Description
Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
ctx.storage.getUrl

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **storageId** (GenericId<"_storage">) - Required - The `Id<"_storage">` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Request Example
```javascript
const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- A URL which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if the file no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/understanding/workflow

Install the Convex npm dependency to your React application.

```bash
npm i convex
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/vue

Install the necessary Convex packages for your Vue project.

```bash
cd my-vue-app && npm install convex convex-vue
```

### Development Deploy Key Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types

Shows the format of a development deploy key, used to provide an agent with full access to a single deployment for development purposes.

```text
dev:joyful-jaguar-123|eyJ2...0=
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Bun project directory

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Use this command to initialize a new Bun project in a dedicated directory.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && bun init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Create Deploy Key Command

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types

Demonstrates the command-line syntax for creating a new deploy key, including optional arguments for specifying the deployment and saving the key to an environment file.

```bash
npx convex deployment token create <name> [--deployment <ref>] [--save-env [path]]
```

--------------------------------

### Create Deploy Key and Capture Output

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types

Example of creating a deploy key named 'my-token' and capturing the generated key into a shell variable using command substitution.

```bash
KEY=$(npx convex deployment token create my-token)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Clerk React SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/clerk

Install the necessary Clerk SDK package for React applications.

```bash
npm install @clerk/react
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy to Preview and Run a Function

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/overview

Deploy to a preview environment and then immediately run a specific Convex function, similar to `npx convex dev --run`.

```bash
npx convex deploy --preview-run myFunction
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Agent Component

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/getting-started

Install the Convex Agent component package using npm.

```bash
npm install @convex-dev/agent
```

--------------------------------

### Create sample data file

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Create a JSONL file with sample task data to import into the Convex database. Each line is a separate JSON object.

```jsonl
{"text": "Buy groceries", "isCompleted": true}
{"text": "Go for a swim", "isCompleted": true}
{"text": "Integrate Convex", "isCompleted": false}
```

--------------------------------

### Get latest timestamp

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/llms.txt

Get the latest timestamp for queries. This is useful for ensuring you are querying the most up-to-date data.

```APIDOC
## GET /public-deployment-api/public-get-query-ts.md

### Description
Get the latest timestamp for queries. This is useful for ensuring you are querying the most up-to-date data.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/public-deployment-api/public-get-query-ts
```

--------------------------------

### Install Faker NPM Package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/query-functions

Installs the @faker-js/faker NPM package, which can be used within Convex query functions.

```bash
npm install @faker-js/faker
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Convex Deploy Key

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/deployment

Demonstrates creating a new deploy key for Convex deployments. Keys can be printed to stdout, saved to a local environment file, or saved to a custom environment file.

```bash
npx convex deployment token create my-token
```

```bash
npx convex deployment token create my-token --save-env
```

```bash
npx convex deployment token create ci-token --save-env .env.production
```

```bash
npx convex deployment token create ci-token --deployment prod
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/nextjs/pages-router/quickstart

Install the convex package to enable Convex client and server functionality in your React app.

```bash
cd my-app && npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Nuxt application

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nuxt

Initializes a new Nuxt project using the official CLI.

```bash
npm create nuxt@latest my-nuxt-app
```

--------------------------------

### Create sample data file

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/nextjs/pages-router/quickstart

Create a sampleData.jsonl file with task objects containing text and completion status for import into the database.

```jsonl
{"text": "Buy groceries", "isCompleted": true}

{"text": "Go for a swim", "isCompleted": true}

{"text": "Integrate Convex", "isCompleted": false}
```

--------------------------------

### Conductor Setup Command

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/agent-mode

Add this command to the `scripts.setup` field in your project's `conductor.json` file to automate per-agent deployment creation.

```json
{

  "scripts": {

    "setup": "npm ci && npx convex deployment create --type=dev my-team:my-project:dev/$USER-conductor/$CONDUCTOR_WORKSPACE_NAME --select"

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### Cursor Local Worktrees Setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/agent-mode

Include these commands in the `.cursor/worktrees.json` file to set up a unique development deployment for each Cursor local worktree.

```json
{

  "setup-worktree": [

    "npm ci",

    "npx convex deployment create --type=dev my-team:my-project:dev/$USER-cursor/$(basename "$PWD") --select"

  ]

}
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Preview Deployment

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/multiple-deployments

Use this command to create a preview deployment with a specific name. It will reuse an existing deployment if one with the same name exists.

```bash
npx convex deploy --preview-name="my-preview-deployment-name"
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Array Validator Example

Source: https://www.convex.dev/llms.txt

Example of defining a mutation with an array argument validator. The array can contain strings or numbers.

```typescript
import { mutation } from "./_generated/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";

export default mutation({
args: {
    simpleArray: v.array(v.union(v.string(), v.number())),
},
handler: async (ctx, args) => {
    //...
},
});
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize ConvexClient and subscribe to updates

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/classes/browser.ConvexClient

Initializes a new ConvexClient instance with a Convex deployment URL and demonstrates how to subscribe to real-time updates for a query using the `onUpdate` method.

```javascript
const client = new ConvexClient("https://happy-otter-123.convex.cloud");

const unsubscribe = client.onUpdate(api.messages.list, {}, (messages) => {
  console.log(messages[0].body);
});
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex CLI

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/overview

Install the Convex CLI globally using npm. This command is required before using any other Convex CLI commands.

```bash
npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Execute query (GET)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/llms.txt

Execute a query function via a GET request. This is suitable for simple queries without complex arguments.

```APIDOC
## GET /public-deployment-api/public-query-get.md

### Description
Execute a query function via a GET request. This is suitable for simple queries without complex arguments.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/public-deployment-api/public-query-get

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **functionName** (string) - Required - The name of the query function to execute.
- **args** (string) - Optional - Arguments to pass to the query function, typically as a JSON string.
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Convex Application

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit

Run the Convex development server to automatically sync your environment configurations to your backend.

```bash
npx convex dev
```

--------------------------------

### Example File Metadata Document

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/file-storage/file-metadata

An example of the JSON document structure returned when querying file metadata from the `_storage` system table.

```json
{
  "_creationTime": 1700697415295.742,
  "_id": "3k7ty84apk2zy00ay4st1n5p9kh7tf8",
  "contentType": "image/jpeg",
  "sha256": "cb58f529b2ed5a1b8b6681d91126265e919ac61fff6a367b8341c0f46b06a5bd",
  "size": 125338
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example OpenAPI Specification (YAML)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/open-api

An example of the `convex-spec.yaml` file structure generated by the `convex-helpers` CLI, detailing the API endpoints and schema.

```yaml
# convex-spec.yaml

openapi: 3.0.3

info:

  title: Convex App - OpenAPI 3.0

  version: 0.0.0

  servers:

    - url: "{hostUrl}"

  description: Convex App API

  ...
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Convex Project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit

Use this command to start a new project with Convex. It will prompt you to select a framework and authentication option, including AuthKit.

```bash
npm create convex@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize ConvexClient in Swift

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/swift/overview

Create an instance of the ConvexClient by providing your Convex backend deployment URL. It's recommended to use a single instance for the application's lifetime.

```swift
import ConvexMobile



let convex = ConvexClient(deploymentUrl: "https://<your domain here>.convex.cloud")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Agent Component

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/components/using

Install the Convex Agent component using npm. This is the first step to integrating new features into your Convex application.

```bash
npm i @convex-dev/agent
```

--------------------------------

### Start MCP Server for a Single Project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/convex-mcp-server

Run the MCP server for a single project by specifying the project directory.

```bash
npx -y convex@latest mcp start --project-dir /path/to/project
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Helpers npm package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/open-api

Install the `convex-helpers` package, which provides a CLI command for generating an OpenAPI specification from your Convex deployment.

```bash
npm install convex-helpers
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Start Middleware with AuthKit

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

Integrate AuthKit middleware into your TanStack Start application by updating src/start.ts. This is required for managing authentication sessions.

```typescript
import { createStart } from '@tanstack/react-start';

import { authkitMiddleware } from '@workos/authkit-tanstack-react-start';



export const startInstance = createStart(() => {

  return {

    requestMiddleware: [authkitMiddleware()],

  };

});

```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Next.js App

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nextjs

Initialize a new Next.js project named 'my-app' using JavaScript.

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest my-app --js
```

--------------------------------

### Example Query Returning Null

Source: https://www.convex.dev/llms.txt

An example of a Convex query that explicitly returns a null value. This demonstrates the use of `v.null()` in schema definitions.

```typescript
export const exampleQuery = query({
  args: {},
  returns: v.null(),
  handler: async (ctx, args) => {
      console.log("This query returns a null value");
      return null;
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Preview Deploy Key Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/deploy-key-types

A preview deploy key is used to deploy code to a preview branch, modifying the behavior of the standard `npx convex deploy` command. This key format includes team and project slugs.

```text
preview:team-slug:project-slug|eyJ2...0=
```

--------------------------------

### Create SvelteKit App

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/svelte

Initializes a new SvelteKit project named 'my-app' using the latest Svelte CLI, prompting for configuration options.

```bash
npx sv@latest create my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Example Document Structures for Union Types

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/schemas

Provides examples of document structures that conform to the union type defined previously, showing both a StringDocument and a NumberDocument.

```json
{

  "kind": "StringDocument",

  "value": "abc"

}
```

```json
{

  "kind": "NumberDocument",

  "value": 123

}
```

--------------------------------

### Install ESLint Plugins for .eslintrc.js

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/eslint

Install the TypeScript ESLint plugin and Convex ESLint plugin as development dependencies for projects using the deprecated `.eslintrc.js` format.

```npm
npm i @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin @convex-dev/eslint-plugin --save-dev
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a New Convex Project Manually

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/using-conductor

Use these commands to manually scaffold a new Convex project before pointing Conductor at it. This sequence is an alternative to letting the Conductor agent handle the initial setup.

```bash
npm create convex@latest my-app

cd my-app

npx convex ai-files install
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex ESLint Plugin for ESLint 9

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/eslint

Install the Convex ESLint plugin as a development dependency for projects using ESLint 9 (flat config).

```npm
npm i @convex-dev/eslint-plugin --save-dev
```

--------------------------------

### Set up Convex Client in Svelte Layout

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/svelte

Initializes the Convex client in `src/routes/+layout.svelte` using `setupConvex` and the public Convex URL, making it available throughout the Svelte app.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">

	import { PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL } from '$env/static/public';

	import { setupConvex } from 'convex-svelte';



	const { children } = $props();

	setupConvex(PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL);

</script>



{@render children()}
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Cargo project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/rust

Initializes a new Rust project named 'my_app' and navigates into its directory.

```bash
cargo new my_app

cd my_app
```

--------------------------------

### StorageReader.getUrl

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageReader

Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

```APIDOC
## StorageReader.getUrl

### Description
Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **storageId** (GenericId<"_storage">) - Required - The `Id<"_storage">` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Request Example
```javascript
const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string | null) - A URL which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if the file no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url_123"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize ConvexClient Instance

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/android/overview

Create an instance of `ConvexClient` by providing your Convex backend deployment URL. It's recommended to reuse this instance throughout your application's lifecycle.

```kotlin
package com.example.convexapp



import dev.convex.android.ConvexClient



val convex = ConvexClient("https://<your domain here>.convex.cloud")
```

--------------------------------

### List Environment Variables with Authorization

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/deployment-platform-api

Demonstrates how to fetch environment variables for a deployment using the Deployment Management API. Ensure you have a valid Convex deployment token and include it in the Authorization header.

```javascript
const token = "ey...0=";

const response = await fetch(

  "https://happy-otter-123.convex.cloud/api/v1/list_environment_variables",

  {

    headers: {

      Authorization: `Convex ${token}`,

    },

  },

);

console.log(await response.json());
```

--------------------------------

### Audit Log Event Schema (Push Config Example)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/integrations/log-streams

An example of an 'audit_log' event, specifically for a 'push_config' action. These events track changes to your Convex deployment.

```json
{

  "topic": "audit_log",

  "timestamp": 1714421999886,

  "audit_log_action": "push_config",

  "audit_log_metadata": "{\"auth\":{\"added\":[],\"removed\":[]},\"crons\":{\"added\":[],\"deleted\":[],\"updated\":[]},..."

}
```

--------------------------------

### GET ctx.storage.getUrl (Deprecated)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageActionWriter

Deprecated: Get the URL for a file in storage by its `StorageId`. Passing a string is deprecated, use `storage.getUrl(Id<"_storage">)` instead.

```APIDOC
## GET ctx.storage.getUrl (Deprecated)

### Description
Deprecated: Get the URL for a file in storage by its `StorageId`. Passing a string is deprecated, use `storage.getUrl(Id<"_storage">)` instead.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
ctx.storage.getUrl

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **storageId** (T extends string) - Required - The `StorageId` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- A url which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if it no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy to Preview, Recreating if Exists

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/reference/deploy

Deploy to a preview environment, deleting and recreating it if a deployment with the same name already exists. This ensures a clean state for the preview.

```bash
npx convex deploy --preview-create "my-feature-branch"
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new Node.js project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nodejs

This command creates a new directory, initializes an npm project, and sets the package type to 'module'.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && npm init -y && npm pkg set type="module"
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Object Type Example and Validation

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/types

Provides an example of a plain JavaScript object and its validation method for Convex. Objects must not have custom prototypes and have entry limits.

```javascript
{a: "abc"}
```

```javascript
v.object({property: value})
```

--------------------------------

### Seed Preview Deployments

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/import-export/import

Seed preview deployments with sample data, often used in CI/CD pipelines like Vercel. This example seeds data from a zip file committed to the repository.

```bash
npx convex deploy --cmd 'npm run build' &&
if [ "$VERCEL_ENV" == "preview" ]; then
	npx convex import --preview-name "$VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_REF" seed_data.zip;
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Clients with Bun

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/javascript/bun

This example demonstrates how to use both ConvexHttpClient for point-in-time queries and ConvexClient for subscriptions and mutations within a Bun environment. It shows fetching messages, subscribing to updates, and sending a new message.

```typescript
import { ConvexHttpClient, ConvexClient } from "convex/browser";

import { api } from "./convex/_generated/api.js";



// HTTP client

const httpClient = new ConvexHttpClient(process.env.CONVEX_URL);

httpClient.query(api.messages.list).then((messages) => {

  console.log(messages);

});



// Subscription client

const client = new ConvexClient(process.env.CONVEX_URL);

const unsubscribe = client.onUpdate(api.messages.list, {}, (messages) =>

  console.log(messages),

);

await Bun.sleep(1000);

client.mutate(api.messages.send, {}, { body: "hello!", author: "me" });

await Bun.sleep(1000);
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex AI Files for Existing Projects

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/using-codex

Run this command in an existing Convex project to integrate Codex. It installs Convex Agent Skills and updates your AGENTS.md file.

```bash
npx convex ai-files install
```
